courses.title: AI & Machine Learning
courses.description: Learn foundations of Neural Networks, Supervised & Unsupervised learning, and Transformers. Work with industry-standard Python deep learning libraries to design, train, and deploy advanced artificial intelligence algorithms.

lectures.title: Lecture 1: Introduction to AI & Machine Learning
Lecture 2: Python for Machine Learning (NumPy & Pandas)
Lecture 3: Mathematics for ML – Linear Algebra & Calculus
Lecture 4: Supervised Learning – Linear & Logistic Regression
Lecture 5: Classification Algorithms – Decision Trees & SVMs

assignments.title: Lab 1: Deploy a Dockerized ML Inference API
assignments.description: Wrap a pre-trained sentiment analysis model in a lightweight FastAPI/Flask application.
assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 10 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.
assignments.expectedOutput: A cURL request to localhost:8080/predict with a JSON payload returns a valid sentiment score.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 3% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

assignments.title: Lab 7: Legacy Code Refactoring Challenge
assignments.description: You are given a poorly structured, deeply nested, and highly coupled snippet relevant to AI & Machine Learning.
assignments.task: Refactor the logic using SOLID principles (or domain-equivalent best practices). Write unit tests first to ensure regression safety, then decouple the components.
assignments.expectedOutput: Clean, modular output that passes all original tests but reduces cyclomatic complexity by at least half.

assignments.title: Advanced Project 14: End-to-End Capstone
assignments.description: Integrate the core concepts of AI & Machine Learning into a comprehensive, production-ready system.
assignments.task: Design the architecture, implement the solution, write automated tests achieving >80% coverage, and prepare a deployment strategy. Include robust error handling and telemetry.
assignments.expectedOutput: A GitHub repository link with a comprehensive README, CI/CD pipeline configuration, and a demo link or video walkthrough.

assignments.task: Design the architecture, implement the solution, write automated tests achieving >80% coverage, and prepare a deployment strategy. Include robust error handling and telemetry.
assignments.expectedOutput: A GitHub repository link with a comprehensive README, CI/CD pipeline configuration, and a demo link or video walkthrough.

assignments.title: Lab 71: Deploy a Dockerized ML Inference API
assignments.description: Wrap a pre-trained sentiment analysis model in a lightweight FastAPI/Flask application.
assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 710 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.
assignments.expectedOutput: A cURL request to localhost:8080/predict with a JSON payload returns a valid sentiment score.

assignments.title: Lab 72: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 1% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

courses.title: Full Stack Web Development
courses.description: Build enterprise-grade full-stack web applications. Covers modern frontend frameworks, scalable backends, database integrations, responsive user-interface designs, security protocols, and state management systems.

lectures.title: Lecture 2: HTML5 & CSS3 – Building Responsive Layouts
Lecture 3: JavaScript Essentials for Web Dev
Lecture 5: Node.js & Express – Building REST APIs
Lecture 8: Full Stack MERN Project Build
Lecture 9: TypeScript for Full Stack Developers

assignments.title: Lab 1: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 10000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 2: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (20 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 30 requests simultaneously, exactly 20 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.title: Lab 43: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 430000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.

assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.

assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 660 requests simultaneously, exactly 440 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

courses.title: Data Science & Analytics
courses.description: Uncover business insights from data. Master statistical analysis, data mining, visual dashboards, data pipelines, predictive operations, and data cleansing architectures using Pandas, Jupyter, and D3.

lectures.title: Lecture 2: Python for Data Analysis – Pandas
Lecture 3: Statistics & Probability for Data Science
Lecture 8: Machine Learning for Data Science
Lecture 9: Building Dashboards with Power BI

assignments.title: Lab 1: Deploy a Dockerized ML Inference API
assignments.description: Wrap a pre-trained sentiment analysis model in a lightweight FastAPI/Flask application.
assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 10 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.
assignments.expectedOutput: A cURL request to localhost:8080/predict with a JSON payload returns a valid sentiment score.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 3% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 830 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.

assignments.title: Lab 86: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 3% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 570 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.
assignments.expectedOutput: A cURL request to localhost:8080/predict with a JSON payload returns a valid sentiment score.

assignments.title: Lab 58: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 2% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 310 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.
assignments.expectedOutput: A cURL request to localhost:8080/predict with a JSON payload returns a valid sentiment score.

assignments.title: Lab 32: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 3% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

courses.title: App Development (Flutter)
courses.description: Create gorgeous native cross-platform applications from single codebases. Master interactive state widgets, navigation routes, async REST integrations, local caches, and build optimizations.

lectures.title: Lecture 1: Dart Programming – Core Syntax & OOP
Lecture 2: Flutter Setup & Your First App
Lecture 6: Working with REST APIs & HTTP
Lecture 8: Local Storage & SQLite in Flutter
Lecture 10: Building & Publishing to App Stores

assignments.title: Lab 1: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 10000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 2: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (20 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 30 requests simultaneously, exactly 20 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.title: Lab 3: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 30000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 4: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (40 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 60 requests simultaneously, exactly 40 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.title: Lab 5: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 50000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.expectedOutput: A GitHub repository link with a comprehensive README, CI/CD pipeline configuration, and a demo link or video walkthrough.

assignments.title: Lab 71: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 710000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 72: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (720 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 1080 requests simultaneously, exactly 720 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

courses.title: DevOps & Automation
courses.description: Architect modern continuous integration and delivery loops. Master Docker containerizations, Kubernetes deployments, GitHub Action automation, and declarative server states via Ansible.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Deploy a Dockerized ML Inference API
assignments.description: Wrap a pre-trained sentiment analysis model in a lightweight FastAPI/Flask application.
assignments.task: Create a Dockerfile that installs dependencies, copies the model weights (approx 10 MB), and exposes port 8080. Build and run the container locally.
assignments.expectedOutput: A cURL request to localhost:8080/predict with a JSON payload returns a valid sentiment score.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 3% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

assignments.title: Lab 44: Handle Class Imbalance in Classification
assignments.description: You have a medical dataset where only 3% of the records are positive for a disease. A naive model achieves 99% accuracy by predicting everything as negative.
assignments.task: Implement SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) or configure class weights in your loss function to penalize false negatives heavily.
assignments.expectedOutput: Achieve a Recall of at least 85% on the minority class on a holdout test set.

courses.title: UI/UX Design
courses.description: Design software screens that satisfy users. Master customer empathy mappings, Figma wireframings, digital design libraries, typography grids, spatial hierarchies, and high-fidelity clickable mockups.

lectures.title: Lecture 5: Color Theory & Typography in Design

assignments.title: Lab 1: Conduct a Heuristic Evaluation
assignments.description: Review a provided wireframe of a checkout flow containing 3 potential friction points.
assignments.task: Identify usability issues according to Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics. Propose structural redesigns for each issue.
assignments.expectedOutput: A PDF report or Figma board detailing the before-and-after states with clear rationale for improved accessibility and conversion.

courses.title: Digital Marketing & SEO
courses.description: Grow business visibility across digital tracks. Learn organic search optimization, pay-per-click engines, social campaign builders, demographic targeting, conversions audit, and performance stats calculations.

lectures.title: Lecture 2: SEO Basics – How Search Engines Work
Lecture 10: Building a Digital Marketing Campaign

assignments.title: Lab 1: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $1000 and a variable cost of $6 per user. The subscription is priced at $25.
assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.
assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $2000 and a variable cost of $7 per user. The subscription is priced at $30.
assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.
assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

assignments.title: Lab 3: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $3000 and a variable cost of $8 per user. The subscription is priced at $35.
assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.

assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.
assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

courses.title: Stock Market & Trading
courses.description: Analyze capital markets systematically. Focus on candlestick patterns, support/resistance lines, economic indicators, derivatives option hedges, risk management equations, and emotion restraints.

assignments.task: Refactor the logic using SOLID principles (or domain-equivalent best practices). Write unit tests first to ensure regression safety, then decouple the components.
assignments.expectedOutput: Clean, modular output that passes all original tests but reduces cyclomatic complexity by at least half.

assignments.title: Lab 36: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $36000 and a variable cost of $41 per user. The subscription is priced at $200.
assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.
assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

courses.title: Content Creation & Branding
courses.description: Construct a loyal public audience. Master message definitions, multi-channel editorial schedules, organic reach algorithms, script structures, visual styling systems, and branding frameworks.

lectures.title: Lecture 4: What Is Branding – Crash Course
Lecture 10: Scaling to a Full-Time Creator

assignments.title: Lab 1: A/B Test Thumbnail and Hook
assignments.description: You have an initial video hook that retains 12% of the audience at 30 seconds.
assignments.task: Write two alternative scripts for the first 15 seconds. One must use an open loop (mystery), and the other must use a direct value proposition. Sketch or describe 2 thumbnail concepts for each.
assignments.expectedOutput: A document comparing the expected psychological impact of both approaches, detailing why one might outperform the other for a broad audience.

courses.title: Entrepreneurship & Startup
courses.description: Transform innovative ideas into scalable business organizations. Master product-market fit parameters, fast prototyping mechanics, business canvas maps, pitch deck designs, and series seeds structures.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $1000 and a variable cost of $6 per user. The subscription is priced at $25.
assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.
assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

assignments.title: Lab 8: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $8000 and a variable cost of $13 per user. The subscription is priced at $60.

assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

courses.title: Communication & Soft Skills

assignments.title: Lab 1: A/B Test Thumbnail and Hook
assignments.description: You have an initial video hook that retains 12% of the audience at 30 seconds.
assignments.task: Write two alternative scripts for the first 15 seconds. One must use an open loop (mystery), and the other must use a direct value proposition. Sketch or describe 2 thumbnail concepts for each.
assignments.expectedOutput: A document comparing the expected psychological impact of both approaches, detailing why one might outperform the other for a broad audience.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Conduct a Heuristic Evaluation
assignments.description: Review a provided wireframe of a checkout flow containing 3 potential friction points.
assignments.task: Identify usability issues according to Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics. Propose structural redesigns for each issue.
assignments.expectedOutput: A PDF report or Figma board detailing the before-and-after states with clear rationale for improved accessibility and conversion.

courses.title: Advanced System Design
courses.description: Architect distributed microservices that scale to millions of users. Master load balancers, caching strategies, database sharding, message queues, and high-availability protocols.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 10000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 2: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (20 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 30 requests simultaneously, exactly 20 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 960 requests simultaneously, exactly 640 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.title: Lab 65: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 650000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

courses.title: Corporate Finance & Accounting
courses.description: Understand financial modeling, balance sheets, P&L statements, and cash flows. Crucial for business analysts, founders, and future CFOs.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Calculate Breakeven and ROI Projections
assignments.description: A SaaS product has a fixed monthly cost of $1000 and a variable cost of $6 per user. The subscription is priced at $25.
assignments.task: Build a spreadsheet or Python script to calculate the breakeven point (in number of users). Plot the projected ROI over 12 months assuming a 5% MoM growth rate.
assignments.expectedOutput: A chart showing the exact month the product becomes profitable, and the total net profit at the end of the year.

courses.title: YouTube Masterclass 2026
courses.description: Master YouTube algorithms, click-through rates (CTR), thumbnail psychology, audience retention hacking, and monetization streams for modern creators.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Channel Branding
assignments.description: Design your channel banner and logo.
assignments.task: Create a cohesive channel banner using Canva.
assignments.expectedOutput: A 2560x1440px banner image.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Thumbnail Design
assignments.description: Create a high-CTR thumbnail.
assignments.task: Design a thumbnail that generates curiosity and uses less than 4 words.
assignments.expectedOutput: A completed thumbnail image.

assignments.title: Lab 3: Keyword Research
assignments.description: Find a search-friendly title.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 10000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 2: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (20 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 30 requests simultaneously, exactly 20 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.title: Lab 64: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (640 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 960 requests simultaneously, exactly 640 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.title: Lab 65: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 650000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.

assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

courses.title: Instagram Reels Virality
courses.description: Crack the Instagram algorithm. Master short-form vertical videos, hook templates, engaging captions, trending audios, and monetization funnels for creators.

lectures.title: Lecture 2: Creating Viral Reels – Hooks & Editing

assignments.title: Lab 1: The Hook
assignments.description: Design a 3-second hook for your Reel.
assignments.task: Write out 3 different text hooks and film a draft.
assignments.expectedOutput: A 5-second video draft with text on screen.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Audio Sync
assignments.description: Sync video clips to a trending audio track.
assignments.task: Create a 15-second Reel synced to the beat of an audio.
assignments.expectedOutput: A published or drafted synced Reel.

assignments.title: Lab 3: Captions & Hashtags
assignments.description: Write an engaging caption with strategic hashtags.
assignments.task: Write a 100-word caption with a CTA and 5 niche hashtags.
assignments.expectedOutput: A finalized caption document.

courses.title: Podcasting 101
courses.description: Launch your own successful podcast. Learn audio recording, microphone setups, interview skills, RSS distribution, and finding your niche audience.

assignments.title: Lab 1: Define Your Niche & Format
assignments.description: Plan your podcast topic, target audience, and episode structure.
assignments.task: Write a 1-page podcast proposal including name, niche, and outline for the first 3 episodes.
assignments.expectedOutput: A clear podcast proposal document.

assignments.title: Lab 2: Audio Recording & Editing
assignments.description: Record a short audio segment and apply basic editing techniques.
assignments.task: Record a 2-minute intro, remove background noise, and add intro music using Audacity or Riverside.
assignments.expectedOutput: A polished 2-minute MP3 audio file.

assignments.title: Lab 3: RSS Feed & Distribution
assignments.description: Set up a podcast host and generate your RSS feed.
assignments.task: Create a free account on a podcast host (e.g. Spotify for Podcasters) and upload your first episode.
assignments.expectedOutput: A valid RSS feed link ready for distribution.

assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 30: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (300 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 450 requests simultaneously, exactly 300 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

courses.title: Rust Essentials (Crash Course)
courses.description: Understand memory safety without a garbage collector! A fast-track course covering Rust ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, and safe concurrency.

lectures.title: Rust Programming – Complete One-Shot Crash Course

assignments.title: Lab 1: Optimize Database Indexing
assignments.description: A database table containing 10000 rows of e-commerce transactions is experiencing slow reads on range queries.
assignments.task: Analyze the query execution plan for 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 500 AND status = "shipped"'. Add a composite B-tree index to reduce the query time by at least 80%.
assignments.expectedOutput: The EXPLAIN ANALYZE output should confirm an Index Scan is used instead of a Full Table Scan (Seq Scan).

assignments.title: Lab 2: Build a Rate Limiter Middleware
assignments.description: Implement a token bucket or sliding window rate limiter to protect a simulated API endpoint from DDoS attacks.
assignments.task: Write a Node.js/Python script that intercepts requests, checks against a Redis-backed store, and returns HTTP 429 if the limit (20 req/sec) is exceeded.
assignments.expectedOutput: When firing 30 requests simultaneously, exactly 20 should return 200 OK, and the rest should return 429 Too Many Requests.

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 6: Your Kubernetes cluster running 60 pods experiences intermittent DNS resolution failures under high load. What is the practical DevOps solution?
options: Switch to physical bare-metal servers
Hardcode IP addresses in the application code
Restart the entire cluster daily
Implement NodeLocal DNSCache

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 7: You are performing PCA on a dataset with 120 dimensions. The first 3 principal components explain only 40% of the variance. What does this imply?
options: You should drop the first 3 components
PCA has failed and should never be used
The data has zero variance
The data is highly complex and not well-represented in a low-dimensional linear space

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 8: While training a deep neural network on a dataset with 8000 samples, you observe exploding gradients. Which technique is a standard mathematical fix for this?
options: Reduce the batch size to 1
Use a linear activation function for all layers
Increase the learning rate to 1.0
Gradient Clipping and Batch Normalization

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 9: A SQL query joining 7 massive tables is causing a full table scan and taking 18 minutes. How do you optimize the execution plan?
options: Convert the database to CSV files
Increase the database storage capacity
Use SELECT * instead of specific columns
Analyze the query execution plan and create composite indexes on join predicates

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 10: Your Kubernetes cluster running 100 pods experiences intermittent DNS resolution failures under high load. What is the practical DevOps solution?
options: Switch to physical bare-metal servers
Hardcode IP addresses in the application code

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 6: A race condition occurs when 30 concurrent threads attempt to increment a shared counter in memory. Which synchronization primitive is most appropriate with the lowest overhead?
options: Atomic Operations / Compare-and-Swap (CAS)
Heavyweight Mutex Lock
Global Interpreter Lock
Sleep(100) before accessing

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 7: Your frontend bundle size has bloated to 10MB, severely impacting First Contentful Paint (FCP). What is the most effective modern strategy to optimize this?
options: Minify HTML only
Serve the app via HTTP/1.0
Implement Route-based Code Splitting and Tree Shaking
Remove all CSS styles

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 8: In a distributed microservices architecture, Service A calls Service B with a timeout of 80ms. Service B experiences latency spikes of 120ms during peak load. Which resilient pattern should be implemented to prevent cascading failures?
options: Synchronous blocking calls
Remove Service B and merge with Service A
Circuit Breaker with exponential backoff
Increase timeout to 5000ms

questions.question: [Moderate] Scenario 9: You notice an N+1 query problem in your ORM while fetching 450 relational records. What is the most optimized approach to resolve this latency bottleneck at the database layer?
options: Increase server RAM
Store all data in an unstructured format
Run a loop and query each record individually
Implement Eager Loading (JOINs) or Batching (DataLoader)

settings.termsOfService: These are the Terms of Service of SkillGenz. All certification exams must be completed independently within the allocated 60 minutes. Sharing answers or utilizing external materials will count as academic dishonesty and result in immediate revocation of any credentials without refund.
settings.privacyPolicy: SkillGenz takes student privacy seriously. Your profile and exam response histories are safely locked under standard credentials and are only accessible by authorized platform administrators and verification audits when recruiters query your credentials.
settings.refundPolicy: If you do not pass an exam attempt, you may select a single retake or purchase additional attempts. Once an exam is launched, we cannot offer any refund for individual exam purchases or premium subscription packages.
settings.disclaimer: SkillGenz is an independent skill verification vendor. While founded by graduates of IIT Madras, we are not an official academic department of the Indian Institute of Technology Madras. Passing certificates verify skills, but direct job placement is subject to partner interviews and placement procedures.
settings.verificationPolicy: Credentials printed with verified unique SV IDs can be securely verified at verify.skillgenz.com by authorized candidates, institutions, or prospective recruiters. Unofficial modifications or tampering with unique QR vectors renders the credentials invalid.
